title: Splunk Certificate Expiration and TLS Trust Chain Health (splunkd server.pem and cacert.pem, Splunk Web, forwarder splunkforwarder.pem, KV Store mongo TLS, cluster manager and peer chains, deployment-server CA, HEC and distributed search peers, SSO callbacks, weak crypto and introspection staleness)
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.1.3 forwarder tcpout connectivity narratives where reachability dominates, UC-13.1.10 search head cluster captain and KV primary election health beyond certificate material, UC-13.1.11 indexer replication fixup stories, generic public web scanning unrelated to Splunk roles, or full certificate lifecycle workflow tools outside Splunk. It does not rotate certificates automatically.
evidence: Saved search platform_uc_13_1_15_cert_expiry_tls_chain with fifteen minute schedule; infra_certs indexes retaining modular input JSON; splunk_cert_inventory.csv with replacement_runbook_url; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab indexer, install a deliberately short-lived server.pem under change control, confirm infra_certs splunk_cert_inventory events show notAfter within seven days, and verify platform_uc_13_1_15_cert_expiry_tls_chain returns critical severity with emergency renewal recommended_action within two fifteen-minute windows.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After replacing the leaf with a ninety-day enterprise certificate and reloading splunkd, confirm days_to_expiry exceeds thirty, severity returns to low or medium, and filtering to critical plus high yields zero rows for thirty consecutive runs when chain_validation_failures remain zero.

dataSources: index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd with component=PEMDataReader or component=SSLCommon for certificate load and trust failures; certificate-adjacent WARN and ERROR lines on splunkd; index=infra_certs sourcetype=splunk_cert_inventory from modular inputs that run openssl x509 against $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/auth/server.pem, cacert.pem, splunkweb cert paths, splunkforwarder.pem, KVStoreSSL material, and operator-declared overrides; index=infra_certs sourcetype=cert_scanner or syslog-forwarded scanner events with cert_not_after; optional REST snapshots of /services/server/messages mentioning certificate expir; lookup splunk_cert_inventory.csv for owner_team and replacement_runbook_url; index=cmdb sourcetype=splunk_role_inventory for CMDB-aligned ownership by cert_id
app: Monitoring Console (built-in) + optional Splunk_TA_nix modular input packaging for openssl collectors
spl:
`comment("UC-13.1.15 Splunk certificate expiration and TLS trust chain health: fuse splunkd PEM and SSLCommon introspection, high-signal certificate warnings, scripted openssl inventory on infra_certs, and external scanner telemetry; enrich with PKI lookup and CMDB cert owners; rolling streamstats by host_role; fleet eventstats by env. Tunables: earliest=-24h@h dte_crit=7 dte_high=14 dte_med=30 min_rsa_bits=2048 stale_introspect_min=360")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-24h@h latest=now (component=PEMDataReader OR component=SSLCommon)
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval host_role=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_role,server_role,"splunkd_pem"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,"unknown"))))
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | eval cert_id=md5(host."|pem|".host_role."|".strftime(_time,"%Y%m%d%H"))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)cn=(?<subject_cn>[A-Za-z0-9.*_-]{1,200})"
      | eval subject_cn=coalesce(subject_cn,"pem_subject_unparsed")
      | eval issuer_cn=coalesce(issuer_cn,"pem_issuer_unparsed")
      | eval sans=coalesce(sans,"")
      | eval not_after_epoch=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(cert_not_after_epoch,not_after_epoch,""))),"%s")
      | eval not_after_epoch=coalesce(not_after_epoch,relative_time(now(),"+120d@d"))
      | eval key_size=tonumber(coalesce(key_size,cert_key_bits,"2048"))
      | eval sig_algo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sig_algo,cert_sig_alg,"sha256WithRSAEncryption"))))
      | eval weak_sig_algo=if(match(sig_algo,"sha1"),1,0)
      | eval self_signed_in_prod=if(match(lm,"self[- ]?signed") AND match(env,"^prod"),1,0)
      | eval chain_validation_failures=if(match(lm,"unknown ca|unable to get local issuer|certificate verify failed|alert unknown ca|hostname mismatch|name mismatch|handshake failure"),1,0)
      | eval cert_introspection_age_min=round((now()-_time)/60,1)
      | eval lane="internal_pem_sslcommon"
      | fields _time host host_role cert_id subject_cn sans issuer_cn not_after_epoch key_size sig_algo self_signed_in_prod weak_sig_algo chain_validation_failures cert_introspection_age_min env lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-24h@h latest=now (log_level=WARN OR log_level=ERROR) (TERM(cert) OR TERM(expir) OR TERM(x509) OR TERM(ssl) OR TERM(tls))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval host_role=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_role,"splunkd_warn"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,"unknown"))))
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | eval cert_id=md5(host."|warn|".strftime(_time,"%Y%m%d%H%M"))
      | eval subject_cn=coalesce(subject_cn,"warn_subject_unparsed")
      | eval issuer_cn=coalesce(issuer_cn,"warn_issuer_unparsed")
      | eval sans=""
      | eval not_after_epoch=relative_time(now(),"+90d@d")
      | eval key_size=tonumber(coalesce(key_size,"2048"))
      | eval sig_algo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sig_algo,"sha256WithRSAEncryption"))))
      | eval weak_sig_algo=if(match(lm,"sha1withrsa") OR match(sig_algo,"sha1"),1,0)
      | eval self_signed_in_prod=if(match(lm,"self[- ]?signed") AND match(env,"^prod"),1,0)
      | eval chain_validation_failures=if(match(lm,"issuer|chain|trust|verify|mismatch|san"),1,0)
      | eval cert_introspection_age_min=round((now()-_time)/60,1)
      | eval lane="internal_cert_warn_error"
      | fields _time host host_role cert_id subject_cn sans issuer_cn not_after_epoch key_size sig_algo self_signed_in_prod weak_sig_algo chain_validation_failures cert_introspection_age_min env lane ]
    [ search index=infra_certs sourcetype=splunk_cert_inventory earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,src_host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval host_role=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_role,cert_role,"cert_inventory"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,"unknown"))))
      | eval cert_id=trim(toString(coalesce(cert_id,md5(host."|".coalesce(cert_path,cert_file,"nopath")))))
      | eval subject_cn=trim(toString(coalesce(subject_cn,cert_subject_cn,"inventory_cn_missing")))
      | eval issuer_cn=trim(toString(coalesce(issuer_cn,cert_issuer_cn,"inventory_issuer_missing")))
      | eval sans=coalesce(sans,cert_san_csv,"")
      | eval not_after_epoch=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(cert_not_after,not_after_iso))),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
      | eval not_after_epoch=coalesce(not_after_epoch,strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(cert_not_after,not_after))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))
      | eval not_after_epoch=coalesce(not_after_epoch,tonumber(cert_not_after_epoch))
      | eval key_size=tonumber(coalesce(key_size,cert_key_bits,"0"))
      | eval sig_algo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sig_algo,cert_sig_alg,"sha256WithRSAEncryption"))))
      | eval weak_sig_algo=if(match(sig_algo,"sha1"),1,0)
      | eval self_signed_in_prod=if(tonumber(coalesce(self_signed,cert_self_signed,0))==1 AND match(env,"^prod"),1,0)
      | eval chain_validation_failures=tonumber(coalesce(chain_validation_failures,chain_fail,0))
      | eval cert_introspection_age_min=tonumber(coalesce(age_minutes,collect_lag_min,round((now()-_time)/60,1)))
      | eval lane="modinput_openssl_paths"
      | where isnotnull(not_after_epoch) AND not_after_epoch>0
      | fields _time host host_role cert_id subject_cn sans issuer_cn not_after_epoch key_size sig_algo self_signed_in_prod weak_sig_algo chain_validation_failures cert_introspection_age_min env lane ]
    [ search index=infra_certs sourcetype=cert_scanner earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,dest,scanner_target,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval host_role=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_role,"cert_scanner"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,"unknown"))))
      | eval cert_id=trim(toString(coalesce(cert_id,md5(host."|scan|".coalesce(cert_serial,"0")))))
      | eval subject_cn=trim(toString(coalesce(subject_cn,"scanner_cn_missing")))
      | eval issuer_cn=trim(toString(coalesce(issuer_cn,"scanner_issuer_missing")))
      | eval sans=coalesce(sans,alt_names,cert_san_csv,"")
      | eval not_after_epoch=tonumber(cert_not_after_epoch)
      | eval not_after_epoch=coalesce(not_after_epoch,strptime(trim(toString(cert_not_after)),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))
      | eval key_size=tonumber(coalesce(key_size,cert_key_bits,"2048"))
      | eval sig_algo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sig_algo,cert_sig_alg,"sha256WithRSAEncryption"))))
      | eval weak_sig_algo=if(match(sig_algo,"sha1"),1,0)
      | eval self_signed_in_prod=if(match(env,"^prod") AND tonumber(coalesce(self_signed,0))==1,1,0)
      | eval chain_validation_failures=tonumber(coalesce(chain_validation_failures,validation_fail,0))
      | eval cert_introspection_age_min=round((now()-_time)/60,1)
      | eval lane="external_scanner_sc4s"
      | where isnotnull(not_after_epoch) AND not_after_epoch>0
      | fields _time host host_role cert_id subject_cn sans issuer_cn not_after_epoch key_size sig_algo self_signed_in_prod weak_sig_algo chain_validation_failures cert_introspection_age_min env lane ]
| eval host=coalesce(host,"unknown_host")
| eval host_role=coalesce(host_role,"unknown_role")
| eval cert_id=coalesce(cert_id,md5(host."|fallback|".strftime(_time,"%s")))
| eval key_size=tonumber(coalesce(key_size,0))
| eval weak_key=if(key_size>0 AND key_size<2048,1,0)
| eval weak_sig_algo=coalesce(weak_sig_algo,0)
| eval chain_validation_failures=coalesce(chain_validation_failures,0)
| eval self_signed_in_prod=coalesce(self_signed_in_prod,0)
| eval days_to_expiry=round((not_after_epoch-now())/86400,1)
| sort 0 host_role host cert_id - _time
| streamstats window=240 current=t global=f last(not_after_epoch) AS roll_not_after last(days_to_expiry) AS roll_days_to_expiry BY host_role host cert_id
| eventstats avg(days_to_expiry) AS env_avg_dte BY env
| eval severity=case(
    days_to_expiry<7 OR self_signed_in_prod==1 OR weak_sig_algo==1 OR weak_key==1 OR chain_validation_failures>0,"critical",
    days_to_expiry<14,"high",
    days_to_expiry<30,"medium",
    true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    chain_validation_failures>0,"remediate_trust_chain_reorder_intermediates_reload_cacert_pem_and_peer_ssl_config",
    weak_key==1 OR weak_sig_algo==1,"reissue_with_sha256_or_p384_and_min_2048_bit_rsa_align_splunk_fips_requirements",
    self_signed_in_prod==1,"replace_self_signed_production_certs_with_enterprise_pki_or_inbound_lb_chain_map_san_for_all_splunk_hostnames",
    days_to_expiry<7,"emergency_renew_reload_splunkd_web_kv_forwarder_cluster_ds_hec_and_distributed_search_peers",
    days_to_expiry<14,"schedule_staged_rotation_validate_openssl_s_client_splunkd_and_monitoring_console_certificate_panels",
    days_to_expiry<30,"open_pki_ticket_validate_acme_vault_or_cert_manager_hooks_confirm_splunk_restart_post_renewal",
    true(),"monitor_inventory_scans_internal_pem_logs_and_rest_server_messages_for_regressions")
| join type=left max=0 cert_id
    [| inputlookup splunk_cert_inventory.csv
     | eval cert_id=trim(toString(cert_id))
     | rename environment AS env_lookup
     | fields cert_id owner_team env_lookup replacement_runbook_url ]
| join type=left max=0 cert_id
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=splunk_role_inventory earliest=-7d@d latest=now
     | dedup cert_id
     | eval cert_id=trim(toString(cert_id))
     | rename owner_team AS owner_team_cmdb
     | fields cert_id owner_team_cmdb ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team_cmdb, owner_team, "unowned")
| eval env=coalesce(env_lookup, env, "unknown")
| eval not_after=strftime(not_after_epoch, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| table _time, host_role, host, env, cert_id, owner_team, subject_cn, sans, issuer_cn, not_after, days_to_expiry, key_size, sig_algo, severity, recommended_action, replacement_runbook_url
description: Detects upcoming and recent expiry of Splunk-issued or operator-managed certificates across splunkd listeners, Splunk Web, forwarders, KV Store members, indexer clustering, deployment server, HEC termination layers, distributed search peers, and SSO callback endpoints while flagging untrusted issuers, name mismatches, weak signature algorithms, RSA keys shorter than 2048 bits, self-signed material in production, and stale certificate introspection telemetry.
value: Platform and security leaders prevent silent cluster fractures, browser trust warnings, and automation outages by unifying internal PEM logs, scripted inventories, external scanner chains, and ownership metadata into one severity-ranked row per certificate with explicit renewal guidance before handshake failures interrupt investigations or customer-facing dashboards.
implementation: Forward PEMDataReader and SSLCommon _internal lanes, land infra_certs splunk_cert_inventory and cert_scanner feeds with openssl-backed paths for each role, maintain splunk_cert_inventory.csv and cmdb splunk_role_inventory joins, save platform_uc_13_1_15_cert_expiry_tls_chain every fifteen minutes with earliest=-24h@h, route critical and high severities to platform crypto on-call with runbook URLs, and archive weekly CSV exports for governance evidence.
visualization: Severity-colored table by host_role and cert_id with drilldowns to infra_certs JSON, _internal PEM lines, and lookup runbook URLs; single-value tiles for minimum days_to_expiry per environment; timeline of cert_introspection_age_min to catch stale modular inputs.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Inventory.device_id) AS device_id latest(Inventory.mac) AS mac FROM datamodel=Inventory WHERE nodename=Inventory.Inv_Device earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Inventory.hostname
| rename Inventory.hostname AS host
| join type=left max=0 host [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Change.object) AS change_object latest(Change.action) AS change_action FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change.All_Changes earliest=-7d@d latest=now BY Change.dest
| rename Change.dest AS host ]
| eval host=lower(trim(host))
| table host device_id mac change_object change_action
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Inventory summaries for Splunk hosts modeled as managed devices when correlating cert inventory to asset identifiers; accelerate Change summaries for certificate rotation change records tagged on Change.dest; retain at least thirty days for quarterly attestations.

references.title: Splunk Docs — About securing Splunk Enterprise with TLS
Splunk Docs — Configure TLS for Splunk forwarding
Splunk Docs — KV Store TLS configuration
Splunk Docs — Manage certificates on Splunk Cloud Platform
IETF RFC 5280 — Internet X.509 Public Key Infrastructure
OWASP — Transport Layer Security Cheat Sheet

knownFalsePositives: Planned grace-window rotations with overlapping validity can look critical until modular inputs catch the new notAfter; require change_ticket correlation before paging application owners. Vendor-issued short-lived certificates that roll every twenty-four hours in integration sandboxes will breach thirty-day thresholds by design; tag those environments as lab in lookups. Test stacks that intentionally use self-signed material will fire self_signed_in_prod unless environment labels downgrade routing. Certificate introspection logs go quiet on idle peers until reload; pair silent PEM lanes with scheduled inventory to avoid false confidence. Penetration scanners sometimes report TLS negotiation quirks on management ports that do not reflect Splunk application failure modes; triage with openssl s_client before treating as outage. Duplicate forwarders double-submit infra_certs events and can skew streamstats until dedupe summaries land. Stale CMDB splunk_role_inventory rows may null owner_team even when PKI lookup is correct; refresh cmdb feeds before muting. Load balancer certificates can renew while origin splunkd certs remain old; suppress edge-only rows only when origin inventory explicitly tracks both layers.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with identity engineering, public key infrastructure operators, Splunk platform SREs, and the teams that manage load balancers plus content delivery edges that terminate customer-visible TLS. This use case is intentionally narrower than forwarder connectivity health cards: it inventories certificates that authenticate Splunk processes to one another and to browsers, measures proximity to notAfter deadlines, and surfaces trust-chain defects before handshakes fail mid-incident. Splunk ships default cryptographic material under $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/auth/ for splunkd listeners, distributed management, and bundled components; many estates replace those files with enterprise-issued server certificates, consolidated certificate authorities in cacert.pem, and role-specific bundles for cluster managers, indexers, search heads, forwarders, deployment servers, and HTTP Event Collector endpoints whether TLS terminates on a load balancer or on splunkd itself.

Ground the deployment in the physical and logical locations of material you must never misplace. splunkd reads server.pem and may pair it with a password-protected private key path declared in server.conf or web.conf depending on whether you protect management, UI, or both. Splunk Web can reference web.conf certPath and privKeyPath pairs distinct from splunkd defaults; a common failure mode after rotation is updating one stanza while the other still points at the pre-migration filename. Universal forwarders and heavy forwarders carry splunkforwarder.pem for outbound cooked TCP when you enable TLS on receiving inputs; the receiving side must trust the issuer that signed that leaf or mutual authentication breaks silently until queues back up elsewhere. Indexer clustering pins peers to a cluster manager with certificates that must align with distributed search and replication expectations; an expired peer certificate can prevent a peer from rejoining even when disk and network look perfect. KV Store on Linux uses MongoDB-style TLS configuration under server.conf KVStoreSSL stanzas with caPath, sslKeysPath, and password files; member certificates expiring without coordinated rolling restarts strand secondaries and stall configuration replication that search head clusters depend on. Deployment servers that sign client certificates or publish a deployment-apps tree with replacement auth material must themselves remain reachable with valid trust during the push window or forwarders retain stale files past expiry.

Distributed search peers added with splunk add search-server or managed through the UI must present names that match subject alternative names on the leaf; alias drift between DNS, certificate common names, and splunkd serverName causes name mismatch errors that resemble network outages. Search head cluster members exchange artifacts with certificates that must include the right mix of client and server extended key usage for your OpenSSL build; rotating only the public certificate without the matching private key produces misleading trust errors. REST callbacks for SAML and OIDC single sign-on terminate at Splunk Web or a reverse proxy; if the proxy presents a commercial chain while Splunk still advertises a self-signed origin certificate to internal automation, scanners report green edges and red interiors. High volume HEC behind a cloud load balancer often presents a public chain at the listener while the origin pool still uses an older self-signed splunkd certificate; both layers belong in inventory with explicit environment tags so operators do not close tickets prematurely.

FIPS mode deployments disallow legacy signature algorithms and short RSA keys; a certificate that looks fine in a browser may still violate FIPS-enabled splunkd constraints. Splunk Cloud rotates many customer-visible certificates as a managed service, yet dedicated stacks and hybrid configurations still require you to upload or validate identity provider metadata, callback URLs, and outbound webhook chains that you own. Intermediate CA ordering matters: leaf, issuing intermediates, then root or cross-signed bridge must arrive in the PEM bundle in the order peers expect; partial chains produce intermittent trust failures that clear when a client caches a different path. Automation with HashiCorp Vault, Smallstep, cert-manager on Kubernetes, or ACME clients should write files atomically and reload splunkd only after file rename completes to avoid half-written PEM fragments.

Risk briefing: planned maintenance with overlapping validity windows should not page like an outage; carry change metadata into lookups. Short-lived certificates from certain vendors expire within twenty-four hours by design for test harnesses; tag those environments explicitly. Certificate introspection logs on idle peers may go quiet until the next reload; pair staleness metrics with scheduled inventory modular inputs. Legal and privacy reviewers should confirm hostnames, organizational units, and customer project codes embedded in distinguished names are acceptable in infra_certs indexes and restricted lookups.

Differentiation recap: UC-13.1.1 remains authoritative for ingestion queues and parsing pressure after bytes arrive. UC-13.1.3 remains authoritative for tcpout connectivity and receiver reachability narratives where TLS expiry is only one contributing factor. UC-13.1.10 remains authoritative for search head cluster captain, KV primary, and deployer bundle health distinct from leaf certificate inventory. UC-13.1.11 remains authoritative for indexer cluster replication factor compliance rather than PEM expiration. This card centers certificate inventory, notAfter proximity, issuer trust alignment, cryptographic strength, and introspection freshness across Splunk roles.

RBAC: grant the saved search role read access to index=_internal on peers that emit PEMDataReader and SSLCommon lines, infra_certs indexes that hold modular input output, optional cmdb indexes for splunk_role_inventory, and rest-based message pulls on a hardened search head. Deny broad human access to detailed distinguished names when policy requires.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane one lands splunkd introspection on index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd where component equals PEMDataReader or SSLCommon. These components emit certificate load narratives, trust failures, and handshake diagnostics that precede user-visible outages. Preserve long _raw lines with adequate TRUNCATE in props.conf on heavy forwarders that relay _internal from peers. Pair with optional event_id=PEMDataReaderConfigPath style markers when your minor version emits them so configuration path mistakes surface early.

Lane two captures high-signal WARN and ERROR lines that mention certificate, expiry, x509, ssl, or tls without requiring a component filter, because some trains log rotation failures under adjacent components. Keep log_level filters tight enough to avoid importing unrelated noise, but loose enough to catch Splunk Web and distsearch adjacency warnings.

Lane three is a scripted or modular input that executes openssl x509 -noout -dates -subject -issuer -serial against canonical paths and writes one JSON event per file into index=infra_certs sourcetype=splunk_cert_inventory. Example paths to scan on Linux include $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/auth/server.pem, $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/auth/cacert.pem, $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/auth/splunkweb/cert.pem when present, $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/auth/splunkforwarder.pem on forwarders, KV Store PEM locations referenced by KVStoreSSL sslKeysPath, cluster manager and peer bundles under instance-specific auth directories, and any web.conf override paths resolved through btool. Windows deployments mirror under %SPLUNK_HOME%\etc\auth with the same logical names. Include cert_path, cert_role, cert_not_after, cert_key_bits, cert_sig_alg, self_signed, chain_fail, and collect_lag_min fields so downstream SPL can compute days_to_expiry without guessing.

Lane four ingests external scanner or syslog forwarded telemetry such as index=infra_certs sourcetype=cert_scanner populated by enterprise scanners or a curated SC4S path that forwards structured cert_not_after fields. This arm catches load balancer certificates that Splunk never reads directly plus perimeter chains that still affect user trust. Normalize timestamps to UTC and include environment, owner_team hints, and scanner_target when the scanned DNS name differs from the Splunk host field.

Optional fifth enrichment uses rest /services/server/messages on a schedule to pull server-side messages mentioning certificate expir when your organization permits automated REST from a management search head. If REST is disallowed in scheduled searches, mirror the endpoint output with an external poller that writes the same schema to infra_certs.

props.conf and transforms.conf should stamp host_role when forwarders know their serverclass, for example indexer, sh, uf, hf, ds, cm, kv, license_master, or hec_lb. Use INGEST_EVAL where supported to avoid post-parse loss. Route infra_certs to a moderate retention index with encryption at rest; separate production and laboratory streams using index-time environment keys rather than free-text guessing.

Create lookup splunk_cert_inventory.csv with columns cert_id, owner_team, environment, replacement_runbook_url, and optional cert_criticality for governance. Populate cert_id consistently with the modular input hash or serial so joins succeed. Maintain splunk_role_inventory in index=cmdb with sourcetype splunk_role_inventory for CMDB-aligned owner_team when PKI lookup rows are sparse.

Example openssl excerpt for modular input design reviews:

```bash
openssl x509 -in "$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/auth/server.pem" -noout -dates -subject -issuer -nameopt RFC2253
openssl x509 -in "$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/auth/server.pem" -noout -text | egrep -i "Public-Key|Signature Algorithm|Subject Alternative Name"
```

Pre-save validation: run index=_internal earliest=-1h for PEMDataReader on an active indexer and confirm events exist; absence implies filtering or wrong host class. Run a controlled modular input against a lab host and verify infra_certs events include sane notAfter epochs.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_1_15_cert_expiry_tls_chain with a fifteen minute schedule in production, dispatch.earliest_time=-24h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after tuning. Throttle duplicate host,cert_id pairs for sixty minutes unless severity escalates from high to critical. Include recommended_action tokens in pager bodies so on-call engineers open the right runbook slice without rerunning five ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans four arms covering PEMDataReader and SSLCommon introspection, focused certificate warnings, modular input inventory JSON, and external scanner lanes so a silent single source cannot hide an upcoming expiry. coalesce() ladders normalize host, host_role, environment, and cert identifiers across naming drift. streamstats provides a rolling per-host_role host cert_id window to stabilize jitter from sparse logs. eventstats averages days_to_expiry by env for fleet context on dashboards. case() encodes severity using proximity thresholds, self-signed production detection, weak signature algorithms, short RSA keys, and chain validation failure counters. join and inputlookup enrich cert_id with owner_team, environment, and replacement_runbook_url, then left join CMDB splunk_role_inventory for authoritative ownership when PKI lookup rows are sparse.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.15 Splunk certificate expiration and TLS trust chain health: fuse splunkd PEM and SSLCommon introspection, high-signal certificate warnings, scripted openssl inventory on infra_certs, and external scanner telemetry; enrich with PKI lookup and CMDB cert owners; rolling streamstats by host_role; fleet eventstats by env. Tunables: earliest=-24h@h dte_crit=7 dte_high=14 dte_med=30 min_rsa_bits=2048 stale_introspect_min=360")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-24h@h latest=now (component=PEMDataReader OR component=SSLCommon)
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval host_role=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_role,server_role,"splunkd_pem"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,"unknown"))))
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | eval cert_id=md5(host."|pem|".host_role."|".strftime(_time,"%Y%m%d%H"))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)cn=(?<subject_cn>[A-Za-z0-9.*_-]{1,200})"
      | eval subject_cn=coalesce(subject_cn,"pem_subject_unparsed")
      | eval issuer_cn=coalesce(issuer_cn,"pem_issuer_unparsed")
      | eval sans=coalesce(sans,"")
      | eval not_after_epoch=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(cert_not_after_epoch,not_after_epoch,""))),"%s")
      | eval not_after_epoch=coalesce(not_after_epoch,relative_time(now(),"+120d@d"))
      | eval key_size=tonumber(coalesce(key_size,cert_key_bits,"2048"))
      | eval sig_algo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sig_algo,cert_sig_alg,"sha256WithRSAEncryption"))))
      | eval weak_sig_algo=if(match(sig_algo,"sha1"),1,0)
      | eval self_signed_in_prod=if(match(lm,"self[- ]?signed") AND match(env,"^prod"),1,0)
      | eval chain_validation_failures=if(match(lm,"unknown ca|unable to get local issuer|certificate verify failed|alert unknown ca|hostname mismatch|name mismatch|handshake failure"),1,0)
      | eval cert_introspection_age_min=round((now()-_time)/60,1)
      | eval lane="internal_pem_sslcommon"
      | fields _time host host_role cert_id subject_cn sans issuer_cn not_after_epoch key_size sig_algo self_signed_in_prod weak_sig_algo chain_validation_failures cert_introspection_age_min env lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-24h@h latest=now (log_level=WARN OR log_level=ERROR) (TERM(cert) OR TERM(expir) OR TERM(x509) OR TERM(ssl) OR TERM(tls))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval host_role=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_role,"splunkd_warn"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,"unknown"))))
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | eval cert_id=md5(host."|warn|".strftime(_time,"%Y%m%d%H%M"))
      | eval subject_cn=coalesce(subject_cn,"warn_subject_unparsed")
      | eval issuer_cn=coalesce(issuer_cn,"warn_issuer_unparsed")
      | eval sans=""
      | eval not_after_epoch=relative_time(now(),"+90d@d")
      | eval key_size=tonumber(coalesce(key_size,"2048"))
      | eval sig_algo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sig_algo,"sha256WithRSAEncryption"))))
      | eval weak_sig_algo=if(match(lm,"sha1withrsa") OR match(sig_algo,"sha1"),1,0)
      | eval self_signed_in_prod=if(match(lm,"self[- ]?signed") AND match(env,"^prod"),1,0)
      | eval chain_validation_failures=if(match(lm,"issuer|chain|trust|verify|mismatch|san"),1,0)
      | eval cert_introspection_age_min=round((now()-_time)/60,1)
      | eval lane="internal_cert_warn_error"
      | fields _time host host_role cert_id subject_cn sans issuer_cn not_after_epoch key_size sig_algo self_signed_in_prod weak_sig_algo chain_validation_failures cert_introspection_age_min env lane ]
    [ search index=infra_certs sourcetype=splunk_cert_inventory earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,src_host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval host_role=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_role,cert_role,"cert_inventory"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,"unknown"))))
      | eval cert_id=trim(toString(coalesce(cert_id,md5(host."|".coalesce(cert_path,cert_file,"nopath")))))
      | eval subject_cn=trim(toString(coalesce(subject_cn,cert_subject_cn,"inventory_cn_missing")))
      | eval issuer_cn=trim(toString(coalesce(issuer_cn,cert_issuer_cn,"inventory_issuer_missing")))
      | eval sans=coalesce(sans,cert_san_csv,"")
      | eval not_after_epoch=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(cert_not_after,not_after_iso))),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
      | eval not_after_epoch=coalesce(not_after_epoch,strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(cert_not_after,not_after))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))
      | eval not_after_epoch=coalesce(not_after_epoch,tonumber(cert_not_after_epoch))
      | eval key_size=tonumber(coalesce(key_size,cert_key_bits,"0"))
      | eval sig_algo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sig_algo,cert_sig_alg,"sha256WithRSAEncryption"))))
      | eval weak_sig_algo=if(match(sig_algo,"sha1"),1,0)
      | eval self_signed_in_prod=if(tonumber(coalesce(self_signed,cert_self_signed,0))==1 AND match(env,"^prod"),1,0)
      | eval chain_validation_failures=tonumber(coalesce(chain_validation_failures,chain_fail,0))
      | eval cert_introspection_age_min=tonumber(coalesce(age_minutes,collect_lag_min,round((now()-_time)/60,1)))
      | eval lane="modinput_openssl_paths"
      | where isnotnull(not_after_epoch) AND not_after_epoch>0
      | fields _time host host_role cert_id subject_cn sans issuer_cn not_after_epoch key_size sig_algo self_signed_in_prod weak_sig_algo chain_validation_failures cert_introspection_age_min env lane ]
    [ search index=infra_certs sourcetype=cert_scanner earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,dest,scanner_target,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval host_role=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_role,"cert_scanner"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,"unknown"))))
      | eval cert_id=trim(toString(coalesce(cert_id,md5(host."|scan|".coalesce(cert_serial,"0")))))
      | eval subject_cn=trim(toString(coalesce(subject_cn,"scanner_cn_missing")))
      | eval issuer_cn=trim(toString(coalesce(issuer_cn,"scanner_issuer_missing")))
      | eval sans=coalesce(sans,alt_names,cert_san_csv,"")
      | eval not_after_epoch=tonumber(cert_not_after_epoch)
      | eval not_after_epoch=coalesce(not_after_epoch,strptime(trim(toString(cert_not_after)),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))
      | eval key_size=tonumber(coalesce(key_size,cert_key_bits,"2048"))
      | eval sig_algo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sig_algo,cert_sig_alg,"sha256WithRSAEncryption"))))
      | eval weak_sig_algo=if(match(sig_algo,"sha1"),1,0)
      | eval self_signed_in_prod=if(match(env,"^prod") AND tonumber(coalesce(self_signed,0))==1,1,0)
      | eval chain_validation_failures=tonumber(coalesce(chain_validation_failures,validation_fail,0))
      | eval cert_introspection_age_min=round((now()-_time)/60,1)
      | eval lane="external_scanner_sc4s"
      | where isnotnull(not_after_epoch) AND not_after_epoch>0
      | fields _time host host_role cert_id subject_cn sans issuer_cn not_after_epoch key_size sig_algo self_signed_in_prod weak_sig_algo chain_validation_failures cert_introspection_age_min env lane ]
| eval host=coalesce(host,"unknown_host")
| eval host_role=coalesce(host_role,"unknown_role")
| eval cert_id=coalesce(cert_id,md5(host."|fallback|".strftime(_time,"%s")))
| eval key_size=tonumber(coalesce(key_size,0))
| eval weak_key=if(key_size>0 AND key_size<2048,1,0)
| eval weak_sig_algo=coalesce(weak_sig_algo,0)
| eval chain_validation_failures=coalesce(chain_validation_failures,0)
| eval self_signed_in_prod=coalesce(self_signed_in_prod,0)
| eval days_to_expiry=round((not_after_epoch-now())/86400,1)
| sort 0 host_role host cert_id - _time
| streamstats window=240 current=t global=f last(not_after_epoch) AS roll_not_after last(days_to_expiry) AS roll_days_to_expiry BY host_role host cert_id
| eventstats avg(days_to_expiry) AS env_avg_dte BY env
| eval severity=case(
    days_to_expiry<7 OR self_signed_in_prod==1 OR weak_sig_algo==1 OR weak_key==1 OR chain_validation_failures>0,"critical",
    days_to_expiry<14,"high",
    days_to_expiry<30,"medium",
    true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    chain_validation_failures>0,"remediate_trust_chain_reorder_intermediates_reload_cacert_pem_and_peer_ssl_config",
    weak_key==1 OR weak_sig_algo==1,"reissue_with_sha256_or_p384_and_min_2048_bit_rsa_align_splunk_fips_requirements",
    self_signed_in_prod==1,"replace_self_signed_production_certs_with_enterprise_pki_or_inbound_lb_chain_map_san_for_all_splunk_hostnames",
    days_to_expiry<7,"emergency_renew_reload_splunkd_web_kv_forwarder_cluster_ds_hec_and_distributed_search_peers",
    days_to_expiry<14,"schedule_staged_rotation_validate_openssl_s_client_splunkd_and_monitoring_console_certificate_panels",
    days_to_expiry<30,"open_pki_ticket_validate_acme_vault_or_cert_manager_hooks_confirm_splunk_restart_post_renewal",
    true(),"monitor_inventory_scans_internal_pem_logs_and_rest_server_messages_for_regressions")
| join type=left max=0 cert_id
    [| inputlookup splunk_cert_inventory.csv
     | eval cert_id=trim(toString(cert_id))
     | rename environment AS env_lookup
     | fields cert_id owner_team env_lookup replacement_runbook_url ]
| join type=left max=0 cert_id
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=splunk_role_inventory earliest=-7d@d latest=now
     | dedup cert_id
     | eval cert_id=trim(toString(cert_id))
     | rename owner_team AS owner_team_cmdb
     | fields cert_id owner_team_cmdb ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team_cmdb, owner_team, "unowned")
| eval env=coalesce(env_lookup, env, "unknown")
| eval not_after=strftime(not_after_epoch, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| table _time, host_role, host, env, cert_id, owner_team, subject_cn, sans, issuer_cn, not_after, days_to_expiry, key_size, sig_algo, severity, recommended_action, replacement_runbook_url
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[platform_uc_13_1_15_cert_expiry_tls_chain_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_1_15_cert_expiry_tls_chain | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch imbalance, materialize each lane hourly into a summary index keyed on cert_id and host, widen operational alerts to earliest=-6h@h on summaries, and retain the full search for investigations. Cap rest fan-out when enabling optional server messages pollers.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on a representative host per role. List configured paths with splunk btool web list and splunk btool server list, then openssl x509 against the resolved files and compare notAfter to the latest infra_certs modular input event. When Splunk shows more optimistic days_to_expiry than openssl, suspect cached partially written PEM files or wrong path selection in inputs.conf.

Cross-check Monitoring Console certificate panels where available and compare to this search for the same host. For cluster managers and peers, validate that distsearch and replication listeners use the intended server.pem and that cacert.pem includes current intermediates after enterprise CA migrations.

Negative test: after a successful rotation with overlapping validity, confirm severity returns to medium or low for fourteen consecutive runs unless other roles still carry stale material. Synthetic positive test: in a lab host, install a deliberately short-lived leaf under change control and confirm critical severity within two windows.

Audit correlation: map alert bursts to change_ticket_id metadata and PKI request identifiers so leadership sees deliberate rotations instead of silent drift. For HEC behind load balancers, validate both the public name presented to clients and the origin certificate Splunk uses for management APIs.

Clock skew distorts days_to_expiry; confirm NTP on collectors and indexers before blaming PKI. If join to splunk_cert_inventory.csv returns null owner_team, fix lookup hygiene rather than muting.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Universal forwarder certificate expired during a renewal failure window: identify the tcpout cluster receiving the forwarder, push replacement splunkforwarder material through deployment server or configuration management, reload splunkd on the forwarder, and validate receiver inputs SSL stanza trusts the new issuer before clearing dispatch backlogs.

Case 2 — KV Store member certificate expired causing configuration replication failure: rolling restart KV-enabled hosts in vendor-supported order, confirm KVStoreSSL paths include full chains, verify mongo log lines return to clean secondaries, and reconcile with UC-13.1.10 timelines if search head clusters stall.

Case 3 — Search head cluster captain cannot validate peer certificates due to chain mismatch: redistribute cacert.pem with intermediates in correct order, reload peers sequentially without breaking quorum, and confirm distsearch SSL names align with certificate SAN entries.

Case 4 — Splunk Web shows browser warnings after rotation because operators installed only the leaf: append missing intermediate PEMs to the bundle referenced by web.conf certPath, reload splunkd, validate with external SSL labs style checks against the customer hostname.

Case 5 — HEC load balancer presents a trusted public chain while origin splunkd still uses a self-signed management certificate: keep both inventories, renew internal origin material, update health probes to match, and document that external green checks do not imply internal automation trust.

Case 6 — Deployment server push of replacement certs failed because endpoints were offline: stage bundles with extended overlap, use serverclass layering to retry pushes, and monitor phonehome queues until all clients report current notAfter epochs.

Case 7 — Cluster manager cannot accept a rejoining indexer peer due to expired peer cert: renew peer server.pem and private key pair, confirm peer cert matches cluster signing expectations, re-add peer under change control, and watch bucket fixup separately per UC-13.1.11.

Case 8 — License master certificate expired breaking distributed stack checks: renew management cert on license master first, reload dependent search heads, and validate license warnings clear in Monitoring Console.

Case 9 — FIPS-enabled deployment rejects weak sha1WithRSAEncryption signatures: reissue with approved curves or RSA sizes, update openssl modular input parsers to flag disallowed algorithms early, and verify splunkd starts clean after replacement.

Case 10 — cert-manager renewed Kubernetes TLS secrets but Splunk pod missed reload: roll pods or exec splunk reload tls after secret mount refresh, confirm new notAfter inside containers matches Vault or cert-manager status.

Case 11 — Certificate missing SAN entries caused alias mismatch for distributed search: reissue with DNS SANs covering fqdn and short names peers use in distsearch stanzas, update serverName consistency, and retest openssl s_client with Server Name Indication.

Case 12 — Vendor self-signed certificate copied from staging into production during a rushed cutover: revert to last known good enterprise leaf, open security exception only with explicit expiry, and rebuild inventory tags so scanners stop confusing environments.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists four arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, multisearch, inputlookup, and join appear; final table lists sixteen analyst columns including owner_team and replacement_runbook_url.
grandmaExplanation: We treat machine certificates like passports at every embassy desk in your estate: when a stamp goes stale, the wrong issuer signs the page, or the name on the ID does not match the door label, trusted hallways suddenly lock. We catch those mismatches early so teams renew and reseal everything before travelers notice the silence.
hardware: Applies to Splunk Enterprise and hybrid stacks on Linux and Windows hosts, Kubernetes-hosted Splunk containers when secret mounts and paths are visible to collectors, and load-balanced HEC endpoints where external scanner lanes capture listener chains distinct from origin splunkd material.
